name: child-agent-ops
description: 子エージェントの起動・指示・報告・安全運用を標準化するスキル。codex でのタスク分解、確認判断、チェックリスト、テンプレートを提供し、リーダー/実装/レビュー各エージェントが迷わず動けるようにする。
capabilities: Define clear roles for leader, implementer, and reviewer agents
Create standardized task decomposition templates for codex
Generate operational checklists for start, execution, and completion phases
Provide communication and reporting templates for multi-agent workflows
Establish safety guidelines for high-risk operations and approvals
Track child agent launch logs with task IDs and completion conditions
author: A-Genno
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidelines for child agent operations. All 20 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: hash values were misidentified as cryptographic weaknesses, Japanese text was flagged as crypto algorithms, and documentation templates with code examples triggered shell execution warnings. No executable code, scripts, network operations, filesystem access, or command execution exists. The skill provides only templates and workflow guidance.
valueStatement: Multi-agent projects often face coordination chaos. This skill provides clear role definitions, checklists, and templates so leader, implementer, and reviewer agents can collaborate efficiently without confusion.
userTitle: Manage child agents with standardized workflows
limitations: Requires codex tool availability for child agent launching
Does not execute code or perform file operations directly
Does not replace human oversight for critical decisions
Cannot automate external system integrations or API calls

useCases.title: Coordinate multi-agent teams
useCases.description: Break down complex projects into parallel tasks, assign specialized agents, and monitor progress through standardized checkpoints and reporting.

useCases.title: Execute delegated tasks
useCases.description: Follow defined scope and completion criteria, report risks immediately, and coordinate with reviewers for code changes.

useCases.title: Ensure safe operations
useCases.description: Enforce pre-approval for high-risk operations like security changes, data deletion, or billing modifications.

promptTemplates.title: Launch Child Agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Launch a codex child agent for [TASK-NAME]. Purpose: [one-line goal]. Completion criteria: [specific measurable outcome]. Estimated time: [duration]. Scope: [boundaries]. Report progress every checkpoint.

promptTemplates.title: Report Task Status
promptTemplates.scenario: Provide progress update to leader agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Report current status for [TASK-NAME]. Current state: [what is done]. Next: [what remains]. Issues: [blockers or risks]. Attach codex launch logs and execution logs.

promptTemplates.title: Request Review
promptTemplates.scenario: Submit code changes for peer review
promptTemplates.prompt: Request review from a reviewer agent. Changes: [files modified]. Risk level: [low/medium/high]. High-risk operations: [none or describe]. Attach implementation summary and testing notes.

promptTemplates.title: Handle Blocker
promptTemplates.scenario: Escalate a blocking issue to leader
promptTemplates.prompt: BLOCKER: Cannot proceed with [TASK-NAME] due to [reason]. Options considered: [what was tried]. Recommendation: [suggested path forward]. Awiting leader decision before continuing.

bestPractices: Always document purpose and completion criteria before launching child agents
Report risks and blockers immediately rather than waiting for completion
Use codex launch logs with task IDs to track agent activities
Require reviewer agent approval for any code changes before merging
antiPatterns: Launching child agents without clear completion criteria
Waiting until task completion to report blockers or issues
Skipping reviewer checkpoints for security-sensitive changes
Allowing parallel updates without leader coordination

faq.question: Which AI tools are compatible with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The codex tool is required for launching child agents.

faq.question: What are the limits on child agent parallelism?
faq.answer: No hard limits, but coordination costs increase with more agents. Leader should balance parallelism against communication overhead.

faq.question: How does this integrate with existing project workflows?
faq.answer: Add the skill to your agent context. Use the provided templates for all child agent launches and status reports.

faq.question: Is data from child agent sessions stored securely?
faq.answer: This skill only provides templates. Storage and security depend on your environment. Attach logs to your existing documentation system.

faq.question: Why is my child agent task not completing?
faq.answer: Check completion criteria clarity. Ensure scope is bounded. Verify the agent has all necessary permissions and tools available.

faq.question: How is this different from standard agent prompting?
faq.answer: This skill enforces structured workflows with roles, checkpoints, and templates. It prevents coordination gaps in multi-agent projects.
